title: Food Donation Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Volunteering & Donating > Food Donation Programs 99 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disaster/Emergency Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness GLIDE REVITALIZATION DONATIONS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts small donations of shelf-stable food, pet food, clothing, small appliances, compact furniture, and household goods. Cannot accept large appliances or big furniture. Supports individuals and families impacted by the March 2025 floods in Douglas County. 301 Glide Loop Drive Glide, OR 97443 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 671-0189 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALLEN TEMPLE EMERGENCY AIDE CENTER EMERGENCY AIDE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides pre-made food boxes and clothing assistance. Closed first week of every month. Also accepts donations of non-perishable food items. 240 Mill Street Sumpter, OR 97877 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOARDMAN FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry and toiletry items to those in need. Food, toiletry, and financial donations accepted. Volunteers welcomed. 314 Locust Road SW Boardman, OR 97818 Eligibility: Unrestricted. One food box per household per week. Hours: Monday 9:30am-11am, Thursday 6pm-7pm Email (541) 219-9535 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT ESTHER'S PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry, pet food, and personal care items that are available once a month. People without an HIV diagnosis may be eligible to receive a one-time food box of pantry staple items. Accepts donations of shelf-stable food, pet food, toilet paper, and personal care items. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 601 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with HIV who have a monthly income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: Monday 1pm-4pm, Tuesday/Thursday 2pm-5pm, Wednesday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 349-4699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES EUGENE CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with clothing, personal hygiene products, diapers, and showers. Helps connect individuals with other community resources and access mainstream benefits, including assistance with SNAP applications and local transit single-ride bus passes (which can be requested twice per month, subject to availability). Also, assists in completing forms to obtain ID and birth certificates, including limited payment assistance. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and other items. 1464 W 6th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with clothing, personal hygiene products, diapers, and showers. Helps connect individuals with other community resources and access mainstream benefits, including assistance with SNAP applications and local transit single-ride bus passes (which can be requested twice per month, subject to availability). Also, assists in completing forms to obtain ID and birth certificates, including limited payment assistance. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and other items. 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP EMERGENCY FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry. Gluten-free items, personal hygiene items, and pet food may be available. Volunteer opportunities are available. 1103 Grand Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Follows USDA income guidelines Hours: Monday-Friday 3pm-4pm Email (503) 325-1702 Get Directions More Details COLTON HELPING HANDS INC COLTON HELPING HANDS FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly shopping-style food pantry. Bring bags or boxes to pack items. Donations accepted and volunteer-based. 30138 S Wall Street Colton, OR 97017 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 10am-6pm (503) 824-2122 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry. Also accepts donations of food. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Residents who self-identify as being low income Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORVALLIS ENVIRONMENTAL CENTER STARKER ARTS GARDEN FOR EDUCATION FOOD FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a program where youth and volunteers grow fruits and vegetables for community members. Produce grown in the garden is donated to local food pantries and social service agencies for distribution. 4485 SW Country Club Drive Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Participants: Low-income families Volunteers: Unrestricted Hours: Hours vary by season Email (541) 753-9211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DALLAS FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry with a 3 to 5 day supply of food. Individuals may visit the pantry once per month and are encouraged to bring their own bags or boxes for transporting food items. Also has pet food. May be able to provide toilet paper, dish soap, laundry soap, and bath soap on a limited basis. Accepts donations of food and pet food. 322 Main Street Suite 180 Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Pantry: Monday-Tuesday/Thursday 1pm-3pm, Wednesday/Friday 9am-11am Food Donations: Monday-Friday 9am-11am Email (503) 623-3578 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DRAIN FISH FOOD PANTRY AND CLOTHING CLOSET DRAIN FISH FOOD PANTRY & CLOTHES CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food and clothing. Also accepts donations of food and clothing. No furniture donations. 128 W C Avenue Drain, OR 97435 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday 9am-1pm Email (541) 836-2212 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ESTACADA AREA FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shopping-style food pantry provides a 5-day supply of food to households. Also offers pet food, hygiene items, household supplies, and clothing as available. 272 S Broadway Street Estacada, OR 97023 Eligibility: USDA-TEFAP income guidelines. SNAP, TANF, SSI, LIHEAP recipients automatically eligible. Hours: Pantry: 2nd and 4th Tuesdays of the month 10am-2pm, Wednesday 2pm-6pm, Thursday 10am-2pm Donations: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm, Wednesday 2pm-6pm Email (503) 630-2888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIRST UNITED METHODIST CHURCH FOOD PANTRY AND COMMUNITY MEAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a food pantry and providesa weekly lunch. Accepts donations of non-perishable foods, pet foods, and hygiene items. 607 W Main Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Coffee and Snack: Wednesday 10:30am-noon Lunch and Food Pantry: Wednesday noon-1pm Donations: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-2pm Email (541) 773-3691 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK CASCADE LOCKS FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of emergency food. Pet food is sometimes available. Donations are accepted. 140 SW Wa Na Pa Street Cascade Locks, OR 97014 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: 4th Wednesday of the month 2pm-6pm and Saturday following 4th Wednesday 11am-noon. Check for holiday hours in November, December, and January. Email (541) 386-3474 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK HOOD RIVER FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of emergency food. Pet food is sometimes available. Donations are accepted. 1130 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday/Friday 3pm-5pm, Wednesday 9am-10am and 3pm-5:30pm Email (541) 386-3474 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK PARKDALE FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of emergency food. Pet food is sometimes available. Donations are accepted. 4910 Baseline Drive Mount Hood Parkdale, OR 97041 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: 1st, 3rd, and 5th Mondays of the month 4pm-6pm; check for holiday hours in December and January Email (541) 352-3500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ROSEBURG ROSEBURG FOOD PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts nonperishable food through a food bag donation program. Participants are provided a reusable green bag, and pick-up is provided. Volunteer opportunities are available. 405 Jerry's Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Bi-monthly second Saturdays of the month (February, April, June, August, October, and December); pick-up times vary Email (541) 672-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOOD FOR FAMILIES DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of shelf stable food items such as oils, flour, canned proteins, spices, and shelf stable milk. Also accepts donations of diapers, dog and cat food, personal and feminine hygiene products, canvas grocery bags, quart and gallon resealable plastic bags, disposable gloves, and produce bags. 3505 SE 182nd Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 220-6679 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOOTHILLS COMMUNITY CHURCH FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry. Also accepts food donations. Donations must have labels and be within the current expiration date. 122 Grange Avenue Molalla, OR 97038 Eligibility: Food Pantry: Low income individuals and families Food Donation: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 9am-3pm, Wednesday 5:30pm-7:30pm Email (503) 829-5101 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRANCIS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-day emergency food supply to individuals and families once every 30 days. Unhoused individuals may come once per week. Also offers used clothing, personal hygiene items, and blankets as available. Guests receiving food are served outside of the facility. Clothing is available inside the center for one guest at a time. Accepts food and clothing donations. 6535 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1:30pm Email (503) 775-6784 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRUITLAND SCHOOL DISTRICT 373 GRIZZLY CORRIDOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry and also accepts food donations. 7233 S Pennsylvania Avenue Fruitland, ID 83619 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-5pm when school is in session Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLADSTONE PARK COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food, clothing, bedding, and linen. Families and individuals can visit the food pantry once per month. Clothing, bedding, and linen offered weekly, depending upon availability. Donations accepted. 8372 Cason Road Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Wednesday 9am-1pm Email (503) 655-4968 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLADSTONE SCHOOL FOOD PANTRY GLADSTONE FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shopping-style food pantry. Families can choose nutritious foods to meet their needs and preferences. Also accepts donations. 6460 Glen Echo Road Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Pantry: Thursday 1pm-5:30pm Donations: Thursday 9am-5:30pm. Donations also accepted Monday-Friday 9am-4pm at Gladstone Community Center (1050 Portland Avenue, Gladstone, OR 97027) Email (503) 737-5283 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLEANERS OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY INC FOOD SHARE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Membership-based food share program. Operates through donations from retailers, wholesalers, distributors, manufacturers, and farms. Each member household shares a portion of food with an approved adoptee. Also operates a clothing center for members. Food and clothing donations welcome. Volunteer opportunities available. 13821 Fir Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Must meet HUD low-income guidelines Hours: Business Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Member Resource Center: Monday-Thursday 8am-1pm Closed for major holidays and severe weather Email (503) 655-8740 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GRACE CHRISTIAN FELLOWSHIP FOOD PANTRY AND CLOTHES CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry and clothes closet. Also accepts donations of food, clothes, and money. 7325 N Bank Street Portland, OR 972035032 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday noon-12:30pm, Thursday noon-2pm, Sunday 11:30am-noon Email (503) 286-2085 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HAWTHORNE PARK POTLUCK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community meal. Volunteer opportunities available and donations accepted. 501 E Main Street Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Friday/Saturday 11:30am-12:30pm Email (458) 592-9233 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE ON THE HILL FOOD PANTRY AND CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A shopping-style pantry and clothing closet (items may include adult and children's clothing and personal care items). One food box or clothing closet visit a month per family. 500 NW Hill Road McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Tuesday 11am-1pm, Thursday 6pm-8pm Clothing Closet: Saturday 10am-noon Donations: Tuesday 11am-1pm, Thursday 6pm-8pm or by appointment Email (503) 472-8476 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW DANCING TREE RESOURCE CENTER WORKER OF THE DAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help navigating resources in the community, information about coordinated access, and information and referral services. Assists on a first-come, first-serve basis with resource information and referral. Supplies fluctuate, but when available may provide Trimet day passes, hygiene items, water/snacks, and clothing. Referrals given for services or items of need that are not accessible through the agency. Donations of unisex clothing and household item are accepted. 10055 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Office: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Donations: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (503) 721-1740 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOIN JOIN DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of food, blankets, sleeping bags, tarps, camping equipment, socks, underwear, hats, gloves, jackets, and hygiene items (shampoo, conditioner, razors, deodorant, feminine products, etc.) 1435 NE 81st Avenue Suite 100 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday 9am-5pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm. Closed on national holidays. Email (971) 710-5768 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOSEPHINE COUNTY FARM COLLECTIVE EMERGENCY FOOD ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly emergency food box. Options include vegetarian, eggs, meat, and vegetables, and shelf-stable items only. Accepts food donations. 485 Daily Lane Grants Pass, OR 97527 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food box requests: Sunday 6am-Wednesday 6am Farm stand pick up: November-April Tuesday 3pm-6pm, May-October 4pm-7pm, Wednesday 8am-5pm locations vary Email (503) 304-3754 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOSEPHINE COUNTY FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Collects and distributes emergency food to a network of local food pantries. Does not provide food assistance directly to the public. Also accepts donations. 3658 Upper River Road Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 479-5556 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH FALLS FIRST UNITED METHODIST CHURCH FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a food pantry that has non-perishable items. Blankets and sleeping bags are available to those in need, depending on donations received. Food donations such as peanut butter, canned meat, cereal, and powdered milk are accepted. 230 N 10th Street Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon Email (541) 884-4053 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING WATER FELLOWSHIP LITTLE FREE PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an outdoor mini food pantry with shelf-stable food items and personal care items; specific items vary and are not guaranteed. 1000 N Main Avenue Suite 12 Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours (503) 842-6455 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANNA HOUSE FOOD PANTRY FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides two weeks worth of food once a month. Bring a box or bags. Food donations are welcome. 88786 Dellmoor Loop Road Warrenton, OR 97146 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 3rd Saturday of the month 9am-11am Email (503) 738-5182 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MY FATHER'S HOUSE MY FATHER'S HOUSE DONATION CENTER DONATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of food, cleaning supplies, paper products, diapers, office supplies, wrapping paper, bus tickets, grocery gift cards, and new toys. 5003 W Powell Boulevard Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Saturday 9am-1pm (503) 492-3046 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORHOOD HOUSE NEIGHBORHOOD HOUSE MAIN OFFICE FOOD SECURITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes that include a mix of perishable and non-perishable items such as fresh produce, canned foods, frozen foods, dairy, and meat. Occasionally offers extra baked goods, which may be accessed at any time during regular business hours. Choices are available for dietary or cultural requirements such as gluten-free, dairy-free, halal, kosher, and easy-to-prepare options. All shoppers may visit the pantry twice a month and may choose up to two weeks worth of food. Delivery is available in select areas. Also accepts food and monetary donations. 8959 SW Barbur Boulevard Portland, OR 97219 Eligibility: Must be at or below 300% of the federal poverty level Hours: Free Food Market: Monday/Wednesday 10am-noon and 1pm-4pm, Thursday 2pm-6pm Donations: Monday/Wednesday 9am-4pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9am-noon Email (503) 246-1663 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORIMPACT REDMOND OFFICE NEIGHBORIMPACT FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Regional food bank that provides food to over 50 partner agency locations that offer food pantries, meals, and free food markets. Does not distribute food directly to clients. Donation and volunteer opportunities are available. 2303 SW 1st Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 548-2380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTH SANTIAM GLEANERS GLEANERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Membership-based food gleaning program that distributes fruit, vegetables, bread, dairy, and other food items as available. Members must commit to 4 hours per month of program related volunteer work. Also accepts food donations. 38952 Highway 226 Scio, OR 97374 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food pickups: Thursday 8am-11:30am New member registration: Thursday 9am-10am Food donations: Thursday 8am-10am Email (503) 910-7407 Get Directions More Details OREGON FOOD BANK FOOD BANK DISTRIBUTION AND DONATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Local distribution center for area food pantries and community food programs. There is no direct service to the general public from the main office. Food donations are accepted. Website Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE DONATIONS AND VOLUNTEERING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of food, personal hygiene products, household cleaning products, diapers, formula, baby powder, wipes, underwear, bras, socks, sleepwear, and cold weather gear. Also offers volunteer opportunities. 10550 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (971) 610-1827 Get Directions Visit Website More Details P:EAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that offers arts and music, mentoring, educational, and outdoor recreational programs. Additional supportive services include bus tickets, haircuts, clothing, and support for pregnancy and parenting. Also offers meals and cooking classes. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and camping gear. 338 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 15-24 experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Friday: 9am-2pm Breakfast at 9am and lunch at noon to those served by p:ear Donations: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Email (503) 228-6677 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PACKED WITH PRIDE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a shopping-style food pantry. Clients may shop once per week. 11305 SW Bull Mountain Road Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday 1pm-3:30pm, Thursday 5pm-7:30pm Email (503) 705-7780 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND OPEN BIBLE COMMUNITY PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry with canned goods, dairy, grains, produce, and meat. Household items such as diapers and wipes, pet supplies, laundry detergent, cleaning supplies, and paper products may be available when supply allows. Availability of items rotate and are provided through donations. Food donations are accepted. 3223 SE 92nd Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Pantry: Tuesday 4pm-7pm, Thursday 10am-1pm except on federal holidays Email (503) 442-8228 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION BURNSIDE SHELTER DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of blankets, linens in new or nearly new condition (twin sheets, comforters, pillowcases), men's, women's, and children's clothing, travel-sized body wash, soap, shampoo, conditioner, deodorant, toothbrushes, toothpaste, razors, and other personal hygiene items. Food items also accepted. No car seats, furniture, or appliances (large or small). 111 W Burnside Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (503) 647-7466 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION SHEPHERD'S DOOR DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of blankets, linens in new or nearly new condition (twin sheets, comforters, pillowcases), men's, women's, and children's clothing, travel-sized body wash, soap, shampoo, conditioner, deodorant, toothbrushes, toothpaste, razors, and other personal hygiene items. Food items also accepted. No car seats, furniture, or appliances (large or small). 13207 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 1:15pm-3:45pm Email (503) 647-7466 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POTLUCK IN THE PARK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community meal, rain or shine. Volunteer opportunities available and donations accepted. NW 8th Avenue and Park on Glisan Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Sunday noon until food is given out (typically 1:30pm or 2pm) Email (503) 255-7611 Get Directions Visit Website More Details REACHING OUR COMMUNITY ROC FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry and food box delivery if available. Accepts food donations. 564 SW Foundry Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Food Pantry: Household income must be at or below 300% federal poverty level, or the household must receive SNAP (food stamps), TANF, SSI, or LIHEAP benefits Delivery: Homebound older adults and people with disabilities that live alone or without transportation Hours: Thursday 10am-4pm Email (541) 476-3344 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIGHT 2 DREAM TOO DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of camping gear (tents and sleeping bags), clothing (socks, underwear, shoes, men’s and women’s apparel), bedding (pillows and blankets), toiletries (soap, shampoo, conditioner, toothbrushes, toothpaste, deodorant, body wipes), food (cooked or uncooked), and operational supplies (55-gallon trash bags, paper plates, plastic cutlery). Cash or check donations can be made onsite to support utility and material needs; receipts are available at the front desk. Financial donations may also be mailed to the registered office or made online.
